messages.content: how would you create a coordinate system to describe mars?

The Martian coordinate system is a way of expressing coordinates on Mars. It is the same as the Cartesian coordinate system, which is used on Earth, but with the origin at the center of Mars. The coordinate system is based on the planet radius, which is 3,389.5 kilometers. The X-axis is parallel to Mars' equator and points away from Earth. The Y-axis points toward the Mars North Pole. The Z-axis points toward the Mars South Pole.
how does a martian degree compare with a earth degree?

A Martian degree is the angle between two lines in a Martian coordinate system. It is roughly 1.5 degrees larger than an Earth degree, which is the angle between two lines in an Earth coordinate system.
i mean, how many kilometers is a martian degree and a earth degree?
A Martian degree is 1/15 of a Martian radius, which is 3,389.5 kilometers. A degree on Earth is 1/15 of an Earth radius, which is 6,371 kilometers.
which means that the circumference of earth is about 3600000 kilometers while the circumference of mars is about 15000000 kilometers?
The circumference of the Earth is roughly 40,075.16 kilometers. The circumference of Mars is roughly 21,930.82 kilometers.
